subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Nerve Damage and Regeneration
description: A 60-second lesson on Nerve Damage and Regeneration.

hook.text: Cut a nerve and you lose feeling. But some nerves can actually grow back—slowly.

buildup.text: Peripheral nerves—in arms, legs, and fingers—can regenerate at about 1 mm per day.

discovery.text: The nerve's outer sheath acts as a guide tube, directing regrowth to the right target.

twist.text: Brain and spinal cord nerves can't regenerate—that's why spinal injuries cause permanent paralysis.

climax.text: Protecting the spine is one of the most critical first aid priorities in trauma.

punchline.text: Arm nerves regrow. Spinal nerves don't. That changes everything.

quiz.question: At what rate can peripheral nerves regenerate?
